channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: FaZe Up Next Unveiled To Discover ‘Pro Gaming’s Future Superstars’
item.content:encoded:
FaZe Clan has unveiled FaZe Up Next, a ‘new generation of FaZe Esports’ in the form of an academy program designed to usher in the next biggest superstars in the pro gaming space. In a post on social media, FaZe posted a cover image of the initiative and promised a fuller announcement coming soon.

In a comment under the post, Kaysan wrote, ‘The future is bright!’ However, fans were expressing mixed sentiments about the new initiative.

The Future of FaZe

In a new post on social media, FaZe Clan uncovered FaZe Up Next:

When fans questioned whether this was technically the first announcement, the team just wrote:

This was the announcement between the announcement and the announcement

In another post, the social team behind FaZe Up Next asked who should be picked up by the label first. However, it’s not immediately clear if this initiative will target a specific game or if it’s an esports-wide effort. The bio on the social profile reads: ‘The home of future cyberathletics superstars.’

In a matter of hours, the profile gathered thousands of followers, all eager to see what the earliest evolution of FaZe Up Next will look like.

FaZe has long nurtured an interest in developing talent, particularly in the esports and content creation spaces. In the last decade or so, we’ve seen challenges like ‘FaZe 5’ surface, giving everyday streamers and pro players the chance to have their voices heard in a very saturated market.

That evolved into FaZe 1 in 2022, which saw FaZe Proze walk into the organization with a $1 million crypto prize, a brand-new car, and a $250,000 partnership with an energy beverage brand. Unfortunately, Proze departed the organization just two years later when FaZe fell into financial distress.

Who do you think should be picked up by FaZe Up Next? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that League’s supports are getting a huge overhaul

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Overwatch on Switch 2 Suffers Huge Performance Issues: “This is a Disaster”
item.content:encoded:
Overwatch has finally launched on Nintendo’s flagship platform, hitting the Switch 2 yesterday, April 14. The long-awaited arrival of the hero shooter hasn’t been without hiccups, though. In recent hours, users have reported the game being ‘completely broken’ on the Switch 2 platform, with some claiming that it’s just the Switch 1 version ‘boosted on Switch 2’.

Blizzard has already acknowledged the issue, writing on social media that the FPS limit on Switch 2 is ‘lower than intended’ and promising a patch to arrive in due course.

Overwatch Hits Switch 2 But Falls Fast

The big selling point about Overwatch landing on the Nintendo Switch 2 is that it offers upgraded visuals and up to 60 FPS performance on the console, which launched almost a year ago. However, players diving into Blizzard’s hero shooter are noticing that’s not the case, as the game is running like mud on the platform.

In a post on social media, Blizzard addressed Overwatch’s launch issues:

We are aware of an issue with the FPS limit on the Nintendo Switch 2 being lower than intended. We are working on a patch to resolve this issue.

In a post on the Nintendo Switch subreddit, users were venting their frustrations about Overwatch’s problems.

One user wrote, ‘Thanks for saving me a download. I’ll give it a month for them to get their s*** together before downloading.’

Another wrote, ‘This is a disaster. It is still 30 FPS, and if anything, it’s a downgrade from the already bad performance of the original client.’

It was just last week that Blizzard announced the imminent arrival of Overwatch on Switch 2, landing alongside the season two update dubbed Reign of Talon. The team wrote:

Take the fight with you as Overwatch arrives on Nintendo Switch 2. Better visuals, higher fidelity audio, and up to 60 FPS in both docked and handheld mode make this a much stronger way to experience the game on your Nintendo Switch 2. It’s a cleaner, crisper, portable version of Overwatch that still lets you play with your friends wherever you are

That has not turned out to be the case, much to the chagrin of players everywhere.

Are you suffering under the weight of Overwatch’s issues on Switch 2? Let us know about your frustrations on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Escape from Tarkov’s new event has been extended

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City Tournament Guide—Schedule, Teams, and Predictions
item.content:encoded:
The BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City is the next big Rainbow Six event in the calendar, landing midway through May 2026. It’s a critical part of the qualification loop that eventually leads to the Six Invitational, one of the most important esports events in the business, and it’ll see the best teams in the world descend upon North America.

Below, you’ll find everything you need to know about the BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City tournament, including the schedule, teams competing, predictions, and where to watch the event.

BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City Schedule and Format

The BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City is set to run from May 8th to May 17th, introducing almost two weeks of intense, competitive action at the highest tier of Rainbow Six esports.

Here’s how the format works:

Play In

  • Eight teams compete in a double-elimination bracket, with four teams securing qualification to the Swiss Stage

Swiss Stage

  • 16 teams compete in a Swiss format, with the top eight teams qualifying for the Playoffs

Playoffs

  • Eight teams face off in a double-elimination bracket (best-of-three), culminating in a best-of-five grand final round

The BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City prize pool hasn’t been confirmed just yet, but hte most recent major toted a pool worth $750,000, with $250,000 being reserved for the winner, so we can assume a similar structure in Salt Lake City.

BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City Teams

The array of teams competing at the BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City hadn’t been confirmed at the time of writing this guide, but the potential teams included the likes of:

  • Twisted Minds
  • G2 Esports
  • Team Falcons
  • Spacestation
  • M80
  • Wildcard
  • Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • FaZe Clan
  • FURIA
  • Weibo
  • CAG Osaka
  • ENTERPRISE

Make sure to bookmark this page and come back to see the teams surface as they qualify for the BLAST R6 Major in Salt Lake City.

BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City Predictions

Without knowing which teams have qualified for the Salt Lake City Major, it’s tough to predict which squad will lift the trophy on the North American stage. If we use precedence, we can see that FaZe Clan just went on a wild run and secured back-to-back Six Invitational trophies, so an easy pick would be the Brazilian organization.

The last BLAST R6 Major winner was M80, using the beastcoast squad that won the BLAST R6 Major in Manchester the year before. However, M80 lost traction at the Six Invitational, resulting in a disastrous elimination in the earlier stages.

We’ll have more BLAST R6 Major predictions as the teams qualify.

How to Watch BLAST R6 Major Salt Lake City

The easiest way to tune into the BLAST R6 Major action when the Salt Lake City tournament goes live is to rely on the official channels:

  • Rainbow Six on Twitch
  • Rainbow Six on YouTube

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’re going to watch the BLAST R6 Salt Lake City Major

For more Insider Gaming esports, check out our guide for IEM Rio 2026

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Tarkov’s Casus Belli Event Extended and New Achievements Added
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has announced an extension of the Casus Belli event that dropped in Escape from Tarkov just a few days ago. If you’ve been pinged in the face by Pillagers too many times, then you’ll need to deal with it for a little longer, as the event now ends on April 20.

It was originally due to end today, April 15, but Battlestate extended the event and added two new achievements, one for each ending of the task line associated with the new event.

Casus Belli is a Solid Tarkov Event

Casus Belli represents one of the best Escape from Tarkov events in a minute. It’s a simple thing that introduces a new faction, Pillagers, and adds a few quests that have started a small war between Skier and Ref. It’s a cross-platform collaboration that ties together both Escape from Tarkov and Escape from Tarkov: Arena, and it’s not that difficult, as events go.

In addition to extending the event, Battlestate Games added two new achievements to Escape from Tarkov, but you’ll almost certainly only get one. That’s a problem for the players who love to complete and unlock everything, but you can only pick one path during this event.

If you side with Ref in Casus Belli, you’ll get the ‘Morituri te Salutant’ achievement, and if you choose Skier, you’ll get ‘Habet, hoc Habet!’

The arrival of the achievements is positive, but not for everyone. Those who gunned forward and completed the task line as soon as it came out didn’t have the privilege of unlocking an achievement at the time, and now, in some twisted way, they feel robbed. They’ll retrospectively get an achievement, but they didn’t know it would be one per path.

Have you managed to complete the Casus Belli task line? Let us know which trader you sided with on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Marathon is getting an experimental mode

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1039)—Answers for Wednedsay, April 15
item.content:encoded:
We’re in the middle of the week by now, and hopefully you’re maintaining that nice, steady NYT Connections streak. If you need help with today’s puzzle, I’ve got the best NYT Connections hints and solutions you’ll find anywhere. They’re waiting for you, just a little further down this page.

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need for today’s challenge.

ow to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 15)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • TASSEL
  • CROWN
  • GOWN
  • HASSLE
  • FLIP
  • GRIND
  • CASTLE
  • FACILE
  • TRIAL
  • CAP
  • TRITE
  • HORSE
  • SHALLOW
  • MITER
  • CHORE
  • DIPLOMA

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Things you’d link with a graduation ceremony
  • Green – Things that might be tedious and boring
  • Blue – Words to describe something basic or simple
  • Purple – Pieces on a chess board

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 15:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 15 (#1039)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – CAP, DIPLOMA, GOWN, TASSEL
  • Green – CHORE, GRIND, HASSLE, TRIAL
  • Blue – FACILE, FLIP, SHALLOW, TRITE
  • Purple – CASTLE, CROWN, HORSE, MITER

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sentinels Partners With Blue Otter For Valorant Game Changers
item.content:encoded:
North American esport organisation Sentinels has partnered with Phoenix-based organisation Blue Otter to support the latter’s Valorant Game Changers roster.

The Blue Otter GC roster will now compete under the co-branded banner of ‘SEN Otters’ for the remainder of the two org’s collaboration.

Sentinels has promised to collaborate with Blue Otter on a merchandise line for the team, while Blue Otter will retain full operational control of the Valorant roster.

Sen Otters will keep the same Blue Otter GC roster, which has been pretty successful this year, with the following lineup:

  • Avery ‘avery’ Eugene
  • ‘Battison’
  • ‘CYN’
  • ‘MAYYY’
  • Allison ‘oasis’ Angel
  • Luka ‘Lolooomir’ Kalanovic (Head Coach)

Together, the roster reached the final of Valorant Game Changers 2026 North America Kickoff, where they lost to FlyQuest RED in the grand final.

Blue Otter GC also finished the GC 2026 NA Stage 1 qualifiers with an unbeaten record and will now compete in the Swiss Stage from April 14.

“It is exciting to see the growth of the Game Changers Valorant competitive ecosystem right now, and Blue Otter’s roster has been putting up results that speak for themselves,” said Rob Moore, CEO of Sentinels.

“A Kickoff grand final and a solid record tells you all you need to know about the level this team is playing at. We wanted to get behind them with real support, resources, and a shared identity,” he added.

Who are Blue Otter?

Blue Otter is a North American esports organisation that appears to have a focus on women’s/marginalised genders and grassroots competition.

As well as the Valorant Game Changers roster, Blue Otter also competes in League of Legends through the Tier 2 tournament, the North American Challengers League.

Blue Otter also had a women’s European LoL team, which competed in LoL Game Changers last year.

Are you excited for Stage 1 of Valorant Game Changers? Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see the big changes to Valorant esports coming in 2027, including updates to the VCT and Game Changers circuits.

MOBILE APP
